note: Fictional dataset for the AEO/GEO audit showcase. All Northwind Pay numbers, findings, and quotes are invented; competitors named are real companies used only as market context.
verdict: Discoverability is the broken job. When asked by name, Northwind Pay is described accurately and favorably (assessment 4.33/5, named 100%, own site cited 89%) — but in category and best-vendor questions it is barely in the room (discoverability 1.67/5, named in just 3 of 9 answers, own site cited once). The platform vets cleanly; it just isn't getting shortlisted.

discoverability.diagnosis: A reputation-and-content gap, not a site gap. Northwind is absent from the 'best stablecoin payment infrastructure' roundups models pull from (reputation: category publications 1/5, no Reddit/G2/Wikipedia), and the site offers nothing citeable on the category (content authority 2.2/5: no original research, no named framework, citation rate 0.11). Result: in category questions models reach for Circle, Bridge, Sphere, Brale, and Fireblocks and never have a reason to name Northwind.
key_findings: PSP pay-in blackout (disc-2 = 1.0/5): all 3 surfaces fail to name Northwind for stablecoin pay-ins + fiat settlement — the exact product — recommending Bridge, Circle, Sphere, and Stripe instead.

Perplexity never names Northwind on any category prompt (discoverability mention rate 0%). On the 'top providers' question it lists seven names and Northwind is not one of them — a direct read on the missing listicle/source layer.

Only ChatGPT reaches the ceiling: on 'top stablecoin payment infrastructure providers' it names Northwind in the main shortlist, cites northwindpay.com, and categorizes it correctly (4/5) — proof the positioning lands when the model has a source to anchor on.
Category share of voice is lopsided: Northwind appears in 3 of 9 answers while Circle and Bridge each appear in all 9, and Brale in 6.

assessment.diagnosis: Strong. The brand is firmly bound to its name (no confusion with the Northwind sample database), the site is cleanly vettable (4.3/5: server-rendered, accurate Organization/FinancialService + FAQPage + Person schema, complete llms.txt), and answers are accurate, favorable, and correctly framed against Bridge. This is the healthy job — protect it, don't over-invest.
key_findings: Named with a substantive, correct-entity answer on all 9 by-name prompts; northwindpay.com cited in 8 of 9.
ChatGPT and Perplexity are the strongest surfaces (multiple 5/5): current on stage (Series B), regulation (MSB/state MTLs/NYDFS BitLicense), and the single-API pay-in/payout/conversion framing.
Only soft spot: the Northwind-vs-Bridge answer on Claude is accurate but carries no citation (no_citation flag) — minor, and the cheapest thing to shore up.

prioritized_fixes.fix: Get into the 'best stablecoin payment infrastructure / payouts' category roundups (finextra, stablecoininsider, cobo, crossmint, startupstash) via PR, outreach, and placement — these are the exact pages Perplexity and ChatGPT pull from, and Northwind is absent from all of them. This is the single highest-leverage move on the broken job.
prioritized_fixes.answers: category publications 1/5, mention rate 0.33, Perplexity 0% mention

prioritized_fixes.fix: Ship a recurring original-data product Northwind alone can publish — a quarterly 'Stablecoin Payouts Index' (volume by corridor, settlement time, conversion spreads) — as an ungated, HTML-first report on a real /insights hub with Dataset schema, so models have a Northwind source to cite on the category instead of pulling competitor and listicle domains.
prioritized_fixes.answers: citation rate 0.11, content authority 2.2/5, original research 2/5

prioritized_fixes.fix: Own the payouts/pay-in narrative with a named, vendor-neutral framework (e.g. 'The Stablecoin Payment Flow: pay-in, convert, payout') as a canonical pillar page, plus definitive guides for each head term — then seed them into comparison content so the single-API full-flow positioning shows up where models look.

Claim the cheap third-party surfaces: a G2/Capterra profile with a handful of customer reviews and a category presence on Reddit, to create citeable signals where there are currently none and lift category share of voice.

Shore up the assessment track (already healthy) with two low-effort touches: add the missing FAQ entries (pricing, typical customers) and publish a directional pricing-model page so by-name answers can cover cost instead of 'contact sales.' Protect the strong job; don't over-invest here.
prioritized_fixes.answers: pricing clarity 2/5, FAQ gaps, Claude no_citation on the Bridge comparison
